name: Blog Starter Demo Data
description: Sample blog posts to demonstrate the blog starter's listing + detail pages.

data.title: Welcome to Total CMS
data.author: Joe Workman
data.summary: A quick tour of what makes Total CMS different — flat-file storage, dynamic routing, and the Site Builder.
data.content:
Welcome! This is your first sample post — feel free to edit, delete, or duplicate it as you build out your blog. Posts are stored as JSON objects in the blog collection, and the Site Builder's page router handles all the URL routing for you.

To add your own post, head to Collections → Blog and click + New Object. Your post will be available at /blog/{id} immediately — no rebuild step.

data.title: Writing Templates with Twig
data.author: Joe Workman
data.summary: How to use Twig in your page templates — variables, filters, includes, and the cms global.
data.content:
Total CMS uses Twig for templating. Inside any builder template, you have access to the cms global — your gateway to collections, configuration, and dynamic content.

Common patterns

  • cms.collection.objects('blog') — fetch all blog posts
  • cms.data.raw('blog', params.id) — fetch a single post
  • cms.config('domain') — read a config value

The Twig Playground lets you experiment with templates without committing changes.

data.title: A Sane Publishing Workflow
data.author: Joe Workman
data.summary: Drafts, publish toggles, scheduled posts — the patterns I use to keep a blog organized.
data.content:
Every blog post in Total CMS has a draft toggle. Draft posts won't appear in your listing or be reachable at their URL. Flip it off when you're ready to publish.

For scheduled publishing, set a future date field and filter by date in your listing template:

{% verbatim %}{% set posts = cms.collection.objects('blog')
    | filter(p => not p.draft and p.date <= 'now' | date('c'))
    | sortCollectionByString('date:desc') %}{% endverbatim %}

That's it — no plugins needed.

data.title: SEO Essentials for Builder Pages
data.author: Joe Workman
data.summary: Meta descriptions, og:image, sitemaps, and the small details that make a difference.
data.content:
The builder-page schema includes everything you need for solid SEO out of the box: title, description, image (used as og:image), and a sitemap toggle.

Total CMS auto-generates /sitemap.xml from your pages and any indexed collections. Visit /sitemap.xml to see it. To exclude a page, toggle off Include in Sitemap in the page form.

data.title: Styling Your Site Without a Build Step
data.author: Joe Workman
data.summary: You don't need npm, Vite, or webpack to ship a beautiful site. Here's how the inline-styles approach holds up.
data.content:
The starter ships with inline <style> tags in the layout — and that's deliberate. Plain CSS still works fine, modern browsers ship excellent layout primitives, and one HTTP round-trip beats a build pipeline for many sites.

When you do want a frontend pipeline, run tcms builder:frontend and you'll get a Vite scaffold ready to go. Until then: edit the <style> block in layouts/default.twig.
